title: panicum crus-galli var. minus
intro: Panicum crus-galli var. minus, also known as Barnyard Grass or Red Millet, is a subspecies of the common reed grass native to Europe, Asia, and Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and supporting the immune system. It can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, with the seeds, leaves, and roots being used separately to harness its medicinal properties.
